frontmatter.Title: The Tavern – A Stranger Arrives
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 micro-want installed (get out before she sees them); T2 interiority and sensory grounding added throughout; T3 clichés replaced with specifics; Rule A/B compliant; no chain weapon (pre-weapon age).

frontmatter.Title: The Training Years Begin
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: get Harald to say the true thing, not the prepared thing; T2 — interiority and sensory grounding throughout; T3 — clichés replaced, summary scenes converted to live scenes; Rule B — begins directly after Ch1 ends (same night, same apartment); no time-skip summary paragraphs; Rule A — single POV throughout.

frontmatter.Title: The City Beneath – First Awareness
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — want: find the thing in the restricted section his father said was there before Soren returns to close; T2 — interiority, sensory grounding, conflicted thoughts; T3 — no summary years, present-tense scene; Rule B — begins three weeks after Ch2 (the training mornings); Rule A — single POV.

frontmatter.Title: First Magic Lesson – Control and Consequence
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: move the stone without visible consequence to the environment; T2 — interiority, sensory detail, self-doubt; T3 — no summary paragraphs; Rule B — begins same season as Ch3 (autumn), several weeks after the Archives visit; first experience of Flux in a live scene.

frontmatter.Title: The First Contact
frontmatter.Tags: [network, Lyssa, Harald, Archives, restricted section, compartmentalization]
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: understand the map before Lyssa takes it back; ticking clock: she has set the paper on the table for exactly as long as she decides to allow; T2 — involuntary counting, physical cold of stone floor, memory of father's map on kitchen wall; T3 — all exposition replaced with present scene; Rule B — linear, age 14, direct continuation from Ch5's post-inquiry period.

frontmatter.Title: The Network's Test
frontmatter.Tags: [network, operation, recruitment, Thrace, Harald, Lyssa, counter-messaging]
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: confirm the counter-information reached the district block before the Covenant officers arrive at 8 AM; ticking clock: 41 minutes; T2 — cold pre-dawn air, candle-shorthand notes, physical stillness as a management tool; T3 — all multi-week summary passages replaced with present-scene grounding; Rule B — linear, age 15, single morning.

frontmatter.Title: The Covenant Scholar
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: understand the full scope of what Soren is asking before his energy fails; ticking clock: Soren's celestial binding worsens over the course of the meeting, losing coherence in waves; T2 — involuntary noticing of symptoms, the physical experience of receiving an inheritance you didn't ask for; T3 — all "over the next year" summary passages cut; Rule B — linear, age 16, single meeting in restricted chamber.

frontmatter.Title: Harald's Final Warning
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: determine whether Harald actually believes they can win or is delivering a rehearsed farewell; ticking clock: Harald has stated he'll need to distance himself once Aisen enters the Academy — two months left, this feels like the last substantive conversation; T2 — involuntary attention to Harald's hands, physical cost of receiving a probability estimate for your own death; T3 — no "the conversation continued" framing; Rule B — single evening, linear.

frontmatter.Title: The Last Ordinary Day
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: see the city as itself one final time before the operational lens becomes permanent; ticking clock: three hours until he must be at the Academy gates; T2 — market smell, the weight of the bag, involuntary cataloguing he's trying not to do; T3 — all abstraction ("the work was about to begin") removed; Rule B — single morning, linear.

frontmatter.Title: Academy Initiation
frontmatter.Tags: [Academy, arrival, dormitory, Cynthia, library, midnight-meeting, network-activation]
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: determine if Cynthia is genuine network or a Covenant probe before sharing any information himself; ticking clock: she'll expect a reciprocal commitment by the end of the meeting; T2 — involuntary assessment processes Aisen is trying to manage; T3 — no "by evening he understood" compressed summary; Rule B — grounded in intake day + evening of day 8, linear.

frontmatter.Title: First Operations
frontmatter.Tags: [Vex, Caris, Archive, network, first-operations, observation, courtyard-meeting]
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 (scene 1) — micro-want: determine if Vex is genuine or a planted Covenant observer before committing to anything; ticking clock: she's already partially read him, decision point before dawn; T1 (scene 2) — micro-want: understand what Archive infiltration means before agreeing to it; T2 — involuntary pattern recognition Aisen is trying to suppress; T3 — all "over the next six weeks" summary passages replaced with compressed bridge; Rule B — linear, two present scenes with temporal bridge.

frontmatter.Title: The Archive Access
frontmatter.Tags: [Archive, Cynthia, Corvin, Section-7, retrofitted-section, Vex, provincial-activity]
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: confirm whether the retrofitted section is genuinely new installation before Corvin closes the session at sixty minutes; ticking clock: 60-minute visit window; T2 — Archive sensory experience (cold, dry preserved air, dust-mineral smell), involuntary cataloguing vs. deliberate observation; T3 — no "over the next three weeks" summary framing; Rule B — linear, first Archive visit + evening coda.

frontmatter.Title: The Competitor Networks
frontmatter.Revision Notes: T1 — micro-want: determine whether Kael has enough operational independence to be trusted with the Archive Section 7 discovery before the midnight bell; ticking clock: Kael has to return before midnight or her absence from the festival becomes visible; T2 — chain-spear competition deliberate underperformance, the physical cost of holding information back; T3 — no "over the next weeks" summaries beyond bridging; Rule B — single present night + brief bridge to coordination established.

frontmatter.Title: Tarovin's Candle Demonstration
frontmatter.AnchorPoints: Tarovin (scholar-cleric), Candle telekinesis

frontmatter_keys: At first the walk was ordinary

Aisen wanted to ask questions but felt small and useless in front of the man who had everything about him suggesting that ordinary questions were unnecessary. Instead he asked the question that had been in his head since their first meeting
A few days later, men on the road—men who had been hunting bandits—brought news
Caspian answered with the same measured voice he'd used before
Aisen watched Caspian and felt something complicated

frontmatter.Title: Winter Snow & Caspian Returns
frontmatter.AnchorPoints: Snow, Lost child, Caspian rescue
frontmatter.At first the walk was ordinary: feet sinking into soft drifts, breath making small clouds. He counted the fence posts like ornaments, one, two, three, until his head bent a little and he lost his usual map. The storm did that—erased landmarks so that the world turned into a repetition of white and white and then the dark of trees like black teeth.
frontmatter.Aisen wanted to ask questions but felt s: "Are you the man who asked what makes someone real?"
frontmatter.A few days later, men on the road\u2014m: there were agents traveling that winter searching for those who hid travelers, and there had been skirmishes on the far side of town. The name of a particular gang came up—a gang that had been responsible for a series of ambushes. Caspian listened quietly to these details and the way they made people move in the tavern.
frontmatter.Caspian answered with the same measured: "A different order. Sometimes force is not necessary."
frontmatter.Aisen watched Caspian and felt something: admiration and fear braided together. Caspian's interventions were not overtly violent, but they rearranged the world just enough to make trouble stop. There was a cost to such rearrangement, Aisen suspected; the man's face sometimes went blank in a way that suggested a piece of himself had stepped away to do the necessary work.
